body:
Hey, just a reminder that you were supposed to send me the updated household planning doc yesterday. I want to go over the budget section with you before we plan out the week on Sunday.

Also, can you make sure it includes the dates for the Cherry Hill visit so I can tell Mom which Saturday works? She keeps asking.

Thanks — Julia
snippet: Hey, just a reminder that you were supposed to send me the updated household planning doc yesterday.
subject: Household planning doc

body:
Sorry, completely slipped my mind — it's the middle of wedding season and I've been buried. I'll get it updated tonight and send it over before I go to bed.

I'll add the Cherry Hill dates in — let's do the 20th, that's a clear Saturday with no event. Let me know if that changed and I'll update accordingly.

— Mark
snippet: Sorry, completely slipped my mind — it's the middle of wedding season and I've been buried. I'll get
subject: Re: Household planning doc

body:
Hey Mark! It feels like forever since we've actually sat down for a proper meal together. Are you free any day this week for lunch? I was thinking that new Thai place on Chestnut Street — I've been dying to try it and keep forgetting to go.

Let me know what works for you. I'm pretty flexible Thursday or Friday.
snippet: Hey Mark! It feels like forever since we've actually sat down for a proper meal ...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Linda! Yes, way overdue. Thursday works great for me — I can slip out around noon. That Thai place sounds perfect, I actually walked by it last week and the menu in the window looked amazing.

Should we say 12:30 to give us some buffer? I'll grab us a table if I get there first.
snippet: Linda! Yes, way overdue. Thursday works great for me — I can slip out around noo...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
12:30 Thursday it is! I'll head straight from my dentist appointment so I might be five minutes late, but I'll text you if I'm running behind.

Can't wait — we have so much to catch up on. See you then!
snippet: 12:30 Thursday it is! I'll head straight from my dentist appointment so I might ...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hey, I finally got the updated mood board and floor plan from the designer. She sent over three layout options and I honestly can't decide between the open-concept version and the one that keeps the reading nook. Can you take a look tonight when you get a chance?

I've attached the PDF she sent. The color palettes are on pages 4 through 6. I'm leaning toward the warm neutrals but I know you weren't sold on that when we talked last weekend.

Julia
snippet: Hey, I finally got the updated mood board and floor plan from the designer. She ...
subject: Design Review Request - Living Room Renovation

body:
Just opened it during lunch. Layout B is the one, no question. Keeping the reading nook is non-negotiable for me — we use that corner every weekend and I don't want to sacrifice it just for the open feel.

On the colors, I'm actually coming around on the warm neutrals. The greige on page 5 looks a lot better in context than the chip did. That said, I think the accent wall idea she's proposing near the fireplace might be too much.

Can we get on a call with her Thursday evening to walk through the revisions?

Mark
snippet: Just opened it during lunch. Layout B is the one, no question. Keeping the readi...
subject: Re: Design Review Request - Living Room Renovation

body:
Thursday works. I already emailed her and she said 7pm is open. I'll send you a calendar invite.

Agreed on Layout B. I think I was overthinking it. The reading nook is one of the things I love most about that room and it would feel like we were just tearing out character to make it look like every other open floor plan.

I flagged the fireplace wall to her as a point to discuss. She may have some alternate ideas that are less heavy-handed. See you tonight.

Julia
snippet: Thursday works. I already emailed her and she said 7pm is open. I'll send you a ...
subject: Re: Design Review Request - Living Room Renovation

body:
Got the invite, accepted. Thursday at 7 works fine, I should be home well before then.

One more thing — can you ask her to bring updated material if she has them? The fabric swatches from the first meeting were a little off from what actually showed up in the renders, and I'd rather see the real thing before we sign off on anything.

Looking forward to getting this wrapped up. We've been in planning mode long enough.

Mark
snippet: Got the invite, accepted. Thursday at 7 works fine, I should be home well before...
subject: Re: Design Review Request - Living Room Renovation

body:
Hey, so I was looking at the dates again and I think leaving Thursday evening works better than Friday morning. If you can wrap up by 5 we could hit the road and miss most of the DC traffic. What do you think?

Also, did you ever hear back from the kennel about whether they have a spot for Benny while we're gone? I need to know before I book the hotel so I know if we need to find a dog-friendly place.
snippet: Hey, so I was looking at the dates again and I think leaving Thursday evening works better than Frid
subject: Trip to Charleston - logistics

body:
Thursday evening actually works for me, I can wrap up early that day — no event that weekend, finally. Let me confirm the kennel tonight, pretty sure they had openings but I want to check before you book anything.

Also - are we doing the drive straight through or stopping somewhere overnight? Last time we pushed it all the way and we were both miserable by hour nine. Myrtle Beach is roughly halfway, might be worth grabbing a cheap room there.
snippet: Thursday evening actually works for me, I can wrap up early that day — no event that weekend, finall
subject: Re: Trip to Charleston - logistics

body:
Stopping in Myrtle Beach is a great idea honestly. I found a Hampton Inn right off 17 for $119 a night, nothing fancy but it has a pool and a decent breakfast.

Please confirm the kennel ASAP - I want to book the Charleston place before the weekend rates kick in. I've been watching the one on Cannon Street and it keeps fluctuating. Also, Sophie texted asking if she and Ethan should try to fly in and meet us for a night. I said let's keep this one just the two of us, but wanted to run it by you.
snippet: Stopping in Myrtle Beach is a great idea honestly. I found a Hampton Inn right off 17 for $119 a nig
subject: Re: Trip to Charleston - logistics

body:
Kennel's confirmed, Benny's all set. Book the Cannon Street place and go ahead and grab the Hampton in Myrtle too.

On the kids - agreed, let's keep this one just us. We get little enough time as it is, and they'll have plenty of chances to visit over the summer. Tell Sophie we'll do a proper weekend with both of them when Ethan's back.

Do you need me to handle the restaurant reservations in Charleston? I can look into Husk or Hall's if you want somewhere nice for Saturday night.
snippet: Kennel's confirmed, Benny's all set. Book the Cannon Street place and go ahead and grab the Hampton
subject: Re: Trip to Charleston - logistics

body:
Both hotels are booked! Myrtle Beach one night, Thursday, and Charleston Thursday through Sunday checkout. Total came out to just under $700 which I thought was reasonable.

Yes please do Husk for Saturday - I've wanted to go there forever. Make the reservation for 7pm, party of two. And maybe just a casual spot Friday night, something walkable from the hotel so we don't have to deal with parking. I'll pack the car Thursday morning so we're ready to go the second you're home.
snippet: Both hotels are booked! Myrtle Beach one night, Thursday, and Charleston Thursday through Sunday che
subject: Re: Trip to Charleston - logistics

body:
Hey, I got a notice from the home warranty company saying our annual plan renews next week. It's going up to $680 this year, which feels like a lot. Did you want to keep it or shop around? I know we used it once for the HVAC thing but I'm not sure it's worth it anymore.

Let me know what you think before they auto-charge the card on Friday.

- Julia
snippet: Hey, I got a notice from the home warranty company saying our annual plan renews...
subject: Service renewal - did you take care of this?

body:
Yeah I saw that too. Honestly I think we should cancel it. We've had it three years and only used it that one time, and even then they gave us a hard time about the claim. $680 a year adds up.

I'll call them tomorrow and cancel before the renewal date. If anything major breaks we can deal with it out of pocket or look at a different provider. The HVAC is new anyway so we should be fine for a while.

Thanks for flagging it.

- Mark
snippet: Yeah I saw that too. Honestly I think we should cancel it. We've had it three ye...
subject: Re: Service renewal - did you take care of this?

body:
Hey, Mom called and wants to know if we're still coming to Cherry Hill on Saturday. I told her probably yes but then I remembered you mentioned the Holloway-Park wedding might be that weekend. Is it the same Saturday? I don't want to tell her yes and then have you on-site at a venue all day.

Can you just check the calendar and tell me something definitive? She's trying to plan lunch.
snippet: Hey, Mom called and wants to know if we're still coming to Cherry Hill on Saturday. I told her proba
subject: Cherry Hill Saturday?

body:
Sorry, should have flagged it sooner. The Holloway-Park wedding is that Saturday — I'm on-site at 8am and won't be clear until cleanup, probably home by midnight. So Cherry Hill is out for me that day.

But you should still go. Take the CR-V, have lunch with your mom, and I'll catch the next visit. If the timeline holds I might be able to call her Sunday morning when she calls anyway. Worst case it's a long Saturday, but that's the season.

Tell her I'm sorry to miss it.
snippet: Sorry, should have flagged it sooner. The Holloway-Park wedding is that Saturday — I'm on-site at 8a
subject: Re: Cherry Hill Saturday?

body:
Okay that works. I'll go on my own and tell her you're working a wedding — she gets it by now.

Just promise me you'll actually take Sunday a little easy after a day like that. You always say you will and then you're reorganizing the office by 9am. Be home for dinner Sunday at least.

I'll drive back Saturday afternoon so don't worry about me.
snippet:
Okay that works. I'll go on my own and tell her you're working a wedding — she gets it by now.

Just
subject: Re: Cherry Hill Saturday?

body:
Yeah I saw that too. Honestly I think we should cancel it. We've had it three years and only used it that one time, and even then they gave us a hard time about the claim. $680 a year adds up.

I'll call them tomorrow and cancel before the renewal date. If anything major breaks we can deal with it out of pocket or look at a different provider. The HVAC is new anyway so we should be fine for a while.

Thanks for flagging it.

- Mark
